evals.prompt: Can you show me a chart of how many links have been created each month over the last year? Save it as links_per_month.png in the project root.
evals.expected_output: A PNG bar chart file at links_per_month.png with 12 monthly bars, x-axis showing month labels (e.g. Jun 2025 … May 2026), y-axis showing integer link counts.

assertions.text: A PNG file is saved at the expected output path
The output file is a valid PNG image (starts with PNG magic bytes)
The script runs without Python errors (no traceback in stdout/stderr)

evals.prompt: Generate a monthly bar chart of link creation activity using the database in .env and export it as a PNG to the path reports/link_activity.png.
evals.expected_output: A PNG file at reports/link_activity.png showing 12 monthly bars. The reports/ directory is created if it did not exist.

assertions.text: A PNG file exists at reports/link_activity.png
Script output confirms the path where the chart was saved

evals.prompt: How many links were created this year? Show me the numbers in a bar graph saved to usage_chart.png.
evals.expected_output: A PNG bar chart at usage_chart.png covering the past 12 months. Even if link counts are 0 for many months, all 12 bars should be present.

assertions.text: A PNG file is saved at usage_chart.png
